summary: Backend PDF generation tested for zh/km/en/id. Chinese fix VERIFIED WORKING (no black boxes, all target chars 发票/租金/电费/水费/押金/总计/状态/待付 render correctly). English & Indonesian unaffected and working. Khmer PDF is BROKEN - STSong-Light is a Simplified Chinese CID font and does NOT contain Khmer glyphs; Khmer Unicode codepoints are being mapped to arbitrary Chinese glyphs (extracted text shows garbled CJK chars like 鰗耗餗鐗輗 instead of វិក្កយបត្រ/ថ្លៃជួល).
passed: Login admin@kostcharge.com works, cookie auth persists
Chinese (zh) PDF: 发票, 租金, 电费, 水费, 押金, 总计, 状态, 待付 all present. NO black boxes / no tofu / no (cid:) markers
English (en) PDF: INVOICE, Rent, Electricity, Water, Deposit, TOTAL, PENDING all present
Indonesian (id) PDF: TAGIHAN, Sewa, Listrik, Air, Deposit, TOTAL, PENDING all present
$ currency symbol renders in all PDFs; billing math correct (Rent $130.00, Electricity 71 kWh × $0.375 = $26.62, Water 2 × $5 = $10.00, Total $166.62)

failed.issue: Khmer PDF renders wrong glyphs (Chinese CJK characters) instead of Khmer script. STSong-Light CID font used for both zh and km (server.py L640: use_cjk_font = lang in ['zh','km']) does not contain Khmer glyphs - Khmer Unicode codepoints get mapped to arbitrary Chinese ideographs.
failed.evidence: pdfplumber text extraction of inv_km.pdf: header renders as '鰗耗耗餗鐗輗' instead of 'វិក្កយបត្រ'; 'ថ្លៃជួល' renders as '逗鬗蜗'; all expected Khmer strings MISSING from PDF text layer. HTTP 200/pdf but content wrong.

action_items: Khmer requires a font that actually contains Khmer glyphs. ReportLab has no built-in CID font for Khmer. Register a TTF such as NotoSansKhmer-Regular.ttf via TTFont (Khmer TTFs are TrueType-outline, so TTFont works - unlike Noto Sans CJK). Then in generate_invoice_pdf branch base_font/bold_font on lang: 'STSong-Light' for zh, 'NotoSansKhmer' for km, Helvetica otherwise.
Alternative: ship the Noto Sans Khmer .ttf under /app/backend/fonts/ and pdfmetrics.registerFont(TTFont('NotoKhmer', 'fonts/NotoSansKhmer-Regular.ttf')) at startup.
